articles.title: Prediction Markets Surged With Bets Just Before Iran Ceasefire
articles.summary: Traders piled into prediction markets with unusually heavy betting activity right before Iran announced its ceasefire, suggesting some bettors had advance knowledge or strong conviction about the timing. The surge highlights how geopolitical prediction markets can become early warning systems for major events, making them valuable intelligence tools for anyone tracking global developments.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Iran Bets Draw Fresh Disputes and Insider Scrutiny
articles.summary: Polymarket faces new controversies over Iran-related betting markets, with questions emerging about potential insider trading and market manipulation. These disputes highlight ongoing concerns about information asymmetries and market integrity that could determine whether prediction platforms maintain credibility with serious money.

articles.title: Are Prediction Markets Gambling?
articles.summary: The age-old question of whether prediction markets constitute gambling is heating up again as regulators scrutinize platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket. The distinction matters because gambling restrictions could kill mainstream adoption, while "information markets" get the regulatory green light that unlocks institutional money.

articles.title: Ethereum’s Silver Medal Slips as Prediction Markets Brace for a $1,500 Floor
articles.summary: Ethereum traders are positioning for a potential drop to $1,500 as the world's second-largest cryptocurrency faces mounting pressure and loses ground against Bitcoin. This floor level represents a critical psychological barrier that could trigger massive liquidations and create profitable arbitrage opportunities across crypto prediction markets.

articles.title: Trump announces Hormuz deadline extension today? Predicti...
articles.summary: Trump appears set to announce an extension of some deadline related to the Strait of Hormuz, the critical oil shipping chokepoint between Iran and the Arabian Peninsula. Geopolitical tension markets will move fast on this news, especially oil futures and Middle East conflict probabilities.

articles.title: An Eddie Murphy comedy shaped insider trading law for prediction markets
articles.summary: The 1983 film "Trading Places" starring Eddie Murphy accidentally created a legal loophole that allowed insider trading on commodity markets, which Congress didn't close until 2010. This obscure piece of financial history now shapes how regulators think about information asymmetry in prediction markets, potentially affecting which events can be legally traded and who gets to participate.

articles.title: Lawmakers call for CFTC crackdown on prediction markets after bet on downed U.S. airmen
articles.summary: Lawmakers are pushing the CFTC to crack down on prediction markets after someone placed bets on U.S. military casualties. This could trigger broader regulatory scrutiny that threatens the entire prediction market ecosystem, not just the obviously tasteless bets.

articles.title: Prediction markets signal confidence in Apple's foldable iPhone
articles.summary: Prediction markets are showing bullish sentiment on Apple finally launching a foldable iPhone, with traders betting the tech giant will join Samsung and others in the bendable phone race. The action suggests bettors see this as more than wishful thinking from Apple fans, potentially signaling insider confidence or leaked roadmap intel that could move AAPL stock when official announcements hit.

articles.title: Polymarket Rebels Still Betting in Banned Countries Using VPNs
articles.summary: Polymarket users in restricted countries are circumventing geographic bans by using VPNs to place bets on the platform. This underground activity shows prediction markets have enough appeal to drive users toward technical workarounds, potentially forcing regulators to either crack down harder or reconsider blanket prohibitions.

articles.title: Kalshi Win in New Jersey Fight Hailed as Big Victory Over States
articles.summary: Kalshi just scored a legal victory in New Jersey, apparently beating back state-level opposition to its prediction market operations. This win could set a precedent that helps prediction markets expand nationwide by showing states can't easily block federally-approved betting platforms.

articles.title: The big state gamble on prediction markets
articles.summary: States are placing massive bets on prediction markets as a revenue source, regulatory framework, or both. If they get it right, we're looking at legitimized mainstream adoption, but if they botch the regulations, it could set the industry back years.

articles.title: House Democrats call on federal regulator to crack down on offshore prediction market war bets
articles.summary: House Democrats are pushing federal regulators to ban or restrict prediction markets that allow betting on wars and military conflicts, likely targeting platforms like Kalshi that operate offshore betting exchanges. The crackdown could signal broader regulatory hostility toward prediction markets, especially those covering sensitive geopolitical events that lawmakers view as inappropriate for wagering.
